clues: This was often depicted in Renaissance art with 'da Vinci Sign', possibly as a stylized beauty ideal or reflection of high social status
As early as 1600BC, Chinese physicians were using seaweed and burnt sponge as treatment options, probably effective due to the iodine content
After salt iodization, this physical examination finding is frequently only seen in autoimmune conditions now
Nucleosides found in the 1950s in a Caribbean sponge, Cryptotethya crypta ultimately led to the development of this drug and approval in 1981
Because this medication is activated only inside affected cells by thymidine kinase, it was the first drug to destroy viruses without harming host cells
Despite being discovered decades ago, this remains the most widely prescribed antiviral for multiple indications

Initially only diagnosed when severe and life threatening with coma, sudden cardiac arrest or renal failure, the advent of new testing has led to most cases being detected in asymptomatic patients now on routine bloodwork
In advanced cases, benign lytic lesion or brown tumors can form often in the jaws or long bones caused by localized rapid osteoclast activity
In the outpatient setting, this is the most common cause of hypercalcemia
The prevalence of this disease increases with age affecting 7% of those >80. Mick Jagger famously was diagnosed with this and underwent treatment at the age of 75
Mortality rates of this are cited at 50-60% within 2 years of symptom onset but if patients undergo surgery, the 2 year survival rises to 90%
Although you might just get an ultrasound now, you can also find characteristic findings using your stethoscope and palpating peripheral pulses

This medication is on the World Anti-Doping Agency's list of banned substances as it can mask the presence of other performance-enhancing drugs although still used extensively in horse racing to treat exercise-induced pulmonary hemorrhage
Available subcutaneously since 2022, this medication is used in about 14% of admitted patients
Acting specifically on the ascending limb of the loop of Henle, this medication replaced less effective and toxic mercury derived options
